title: THETA Control Room — Build Hook Delta

changedProjects.lastKnownStatus: Build hook reported latest project state.
changedProjects.nextAction: v0.4.34 — CLEAN-HQ OVERSAMPLER + LO-FI RELABEL (user: oversampling rolls off highs). MEASURED ROOT CAUSE: the VINTAGE OS decimator is [1,2,1]/4 (cos^2) — -2dB@10k/-15dB@20k at 44.1k AND alias rejection that WORSENS at 4x/8x (-44 -> -29dB); it is a lo-fi character, not a quality tool. The 47-tap CLEAN was flat at 48k+ but -0.4dB@18k/-3dB@20k at 44.1k. FIX 1: new kCleanHQ119 (119-tap Kaiser-beta8 min-phase halfband, cepstral factorization) as the BASE-RATE stage only (deeper stages keep 47 taps — their bands sit far above audio): measured +/-0.002dB to 20kHz@44.1k, -80.5dB stopband, -98dB alias @4x/8x, unity DC, ~4-base-smp latency (vs 3). Gated GRIT>=6 / AMBER>=3 / COMPRESS>=3 (DSPVersion bumps; older states keep the frozen 47-tap CLEAN bit-identically; keyless sessions still freeze old). All 23 shipped presets restamped 6/3/3. FIX 2: TWEAK OS CHARACTER label VINTAGE -> LO-FI so nobody mistakes it for a quality setting. VERIFY IN ABLETON: at 44.1k with CLEAN, toggling 2x/4x/8x should no longer dull the top end at all; LO-FI still sounds like the old vintage grit; old sessions render unchanged. NOTE: on CLEAN, 2x is transparent; 4x/8x only add alias margin under heavy drive. OPEN: painted-metal chassis; deferred patina; LIMITER rename.
